turns.user_message: I tried to clear my head by going for a long walk and listening to my favorite playlist, but it just ended up making me feel worse. I kept replaying every awkward thing I said yesterday instead of actually paying attention to the music. It feels so stupid that I can't even do something simple to lift my own mood without overthinking it. I just end up sitting on the couch afterward, completely drained and annoyed at myself for wasting the afternoon.

I keep trying these little routines to reset my mindset, but they always spiral into the exact same heavy feeling. I buy the nice coffee, I set up the cozy reading nook, and I still just sit there staring at the wall while my brain picks apart every mistake I made last week. It’s exhausting to watch myself fail at basic downtime over and over again. I start to believe that I’m just broken in a way that no amount of planning can actually fix.

I honestly don’t know why I keep pretending that changing my environment will magically wash away this constant sense of disappointment. I rearrange the furniture, I force myself to take a long bath, and I still end up feeling completely hollow and ashamed of my own thoughts. It feels like I am actively poisoning every single moment of peace I try to carve out for myself. I’m so tired of being the one who ruins my own quiet time and leaves me feeling worse than before.

generation_metadata.diversity_anchor: The speaker opens with a failed attempt to feel better.
